archive.title: Set up ROS2
archive.estimate:
Goal is to show that the pixhawk can receive and act on commands from the jetson. Intent is to show up/down/left/right being commanded via the jetson only. No RC
-Get ROS running - Complete, seems like I got it running, Eitan says that I shouldn't worry about CUDA and all that stuff for now
-Understand how my stuff will fit into ROS2- Complete: CGPT is very helpful. I also made a onenote that walks through process of building nodes and subscribers
->Get flight control script into node, demonstrate that I can echo data output - This should work but am running into an error wherein flightlog.py line 36 is messing everything up. I'm not sure why it's incomplete but function update register is has a for loop w/ nothing in it. this needs to get fixe in order to run
- fix flight_log.py, line 36 update_register() - Complete: fixed and a long time later, was able to get it to run and publish. Noted that the altitude value that it is broadcasting  seems not too far off. although it should be recording as function of it's takeoff point currently recording ~ .2 m. I also have this error where it has to publish as a float, not int. that said, it was unable to publish the actual velocity and attitude values. I might need to explicitly specify float at the formation of the array
->Fix the issue w/ the array not publishing floats so that it can publish the actual values from pixhawk
archive.notes: Complete

archive.title: Determine if my current ROS + vehicle setup makes sense
archive.estimate:
It occurs to me that this set up doesn't make a whole lot of sense in the context of ROS. Perhaps take the important parts and create a new environment with new nodes.
-> See if I can optimize this by creating a new code structure that is better suited to ROS - In progress being done on Athena because myriad got damaged. - Currently held up because the new pixhawk isn't arming because it's not set up yet. When working on this, was having issue because the pixhawks RC port wasn't outputting the full voltage. Unsure why but this needs to get fixed
-Figure out the low voltage issue - Figured this out. Turned out to be an issue w/ my using the old mSD card w/o downloading the firmware. It now works
->finish setting up the pixhawk - Need to set up the radio, currently experiencing the same difficulty I've had every time I've set up the radio - Complete,  915 mHz works with 915 mHz
- Setting this task to complete and will continue with more granular tasks. The answer is that "yes",best practice is to break ROS up into servers
archive.notes: Complete

archive.title: Demonstrate capability to publish and receive data
archive.estimate:
I've demonstrated the capability to publish and can showed that I can echo back items in a split terminal. That said, I haven't demonstrated capability to recieve data as a useable value and I haven't published all items that I'd like to publish 
-Go and see if I can build a separate subscriber and log the data from my flight node publishers - Complete: produced a dummy node called target_track that publishes dummy xy dxdy and ddxddy data and showed that flight node is able to read and print the values from target_track
-> Go in and make sure I'm publishing all data that I'd like to publish. this includes:
-- Attitude - Complete
-- position estimate - working on this w/ complimentary filter for determining z, currently got an error looking like an incorrect datatype was passed - Complete
-- acceleration - Complete including angular acceleration
-- velocity - Complete
-->> Commands - Complete, now outputs command values
archive.notes: Complete

graveyard.title: Build method for keeping time only during consistent tracks
graveyard.estimate:
Need the I gain to not explode in the time between tracks. 
-> build in logic that the timer starts w/ each new track and that, whenever the track is lost for a certain amount of time, the timer starts over - Started this: I added some logic to telemetered_flight light 108-ish to count how many iterations it didn't get a track, if the number is 3 or more, then it sets controller.track to 0, the next step is to go into PID and add in logic that says that if the track is true, then take time and calculate dt based on the reference time. If the track is false, then simply log the reference time (? maybe) . need it to only keep the dt during consistent tracks. perhaps need it to set the PID_time to empty if the track is no longer consistent
- add in logic to PID class to act on whether the track is consistent - Complete: added this logic and verified that it works by printing out the values values and showing that they revert to an empty vector when track is lost
